response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior software engineer with strong web/backend credentials, but the role specifically requires a C++ developer with 8-12 years building high-performance on-premises and cloud platform solutions, industrial automation exposure, and IEC 62443 secure development experience. The resume shows only a brief 6-month stint writing C/C++ components at National Instruments (2014-2015); the rest of the career is dominated by Node.js, TypeScript, DLang, and web/full-stack work. There is no evidence of message broker architectures, MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850/104, DNP 3.0), or SDL/IEC 62443 practice. Skill mismatch is too large for this specialized C++/industrial role.
strengths: Senior-level experience (~13 years) with strong software craftsmanship, TDD, and CI/CD discipline
Proven backend and distributed systems work, including low-latency microservices handling millions of requests/sec
Some prior C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and C++ usage in a wave-field-synthesis project
concerns: C++ is not a current or primary skill — only ~6 months of professional C++ work over a decade ago
No industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, or industrial protocol (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA) experience demonstrated
No evidence of message-broker-based architectures or IoT platform development required by the role

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or IEC 62443 / SDL background
Strong general senior engineering and distributed-systems track record
